It's too slow to respond, you have to scream to get it to answer, a constantly loses connection with the smart plugs, it doesn't interact well with Apple TV, whenever I ask a question it tells me to ask it again and unlock my phone. My Alexa echo dot always worked great, always responded quickly, never lost contact with the smart plugs and always answered all my questions, without having to go to my Alexa app on my phone! If there was a way I could get a refund for this piece of crap, I wish I could!

title: HomePod Mini vs Echo Dot Max: Don’t Pick Wrong!
text:
Seeing as the HomePod is a totally useless paperweight for anyone who doesn't own an iPhone, Alexa (Echo) will win the global market share by an absolute landslide. Of course it already has. But the fact remains that the Amazon Echo is just a more capable device, generally speaking. It has better support and is available in more countries, too. The HomePod still has too many limitations to be taken seriously by most people. Outside of a few niche customers, it just won't compete against the Amazon Echo. Not to mention the vast majority of people interested in home assistant speakers have already solidified their place in the Amazon ecosystem a very long time ago. Most people can't be bothered to switch. As we have seen time and time again, once people invest in an ecosystem, they seldom leave. 

The HomePod fills a niche for iPhone owners. But it will never be a mainstream device like the Amazon or Google ecosystems are.

title: HomePod Mini vs Echo Dot Max: Don’t Pick Wrong!
text: Just switched one of my Alexa Echos to a HomePod mini hoping to switch all my Alexas to HomePods. But my biggest beef with the HomePods is how useless they are for reminders. I give Alexa reminders all day in my kitchen and office to alert me audibly. Those reminders are lifesavers. Alexa repeats the reminders aloud a few times and sends me a text. Wonderful. HomePods just send the reminder to my phone (which is always on silent).  Pretty useless when I'm in the kitchen working with multiple dishes and just want an audible reminder like, "Check on the stuffing." The reminder quietly goes to my phone which I'm not looking at, so I miss it entirely. Same on the job. Phone is tucked away, and the reminder to, "Get ready for my Zoom" goes silently to my phone when I really just want HomePod to announce it aloud to get my attention like Alexa's reminders do. I can set a timer, but it just doesn't compare to how useful the Alexa reminders are. I really don't think I can convert to HomePods until/unless reminders are improved because I rely on the audible reminders multiple times a day. I reeeeallly hope Apple improves this because I want to ditch Amazon/Alexa.
